items.title: 10 Best Turn-Based RPGs of All Time, Ranked
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dragon Quest XI some of the greatest turn-based RPGs ever.
items.content:
clair obscur expedition 33 featured art Image via Sandfall Interactive By Sage Ashford Published Jul 18, 2026, 10:45 PM EDT Staff Writer for CBR, Sage Ashford has also written for Comicon as well as other sites such as The Gamer, and has been doing freelance work since 2014, and been working for CBR since 2016. His focus is primarily on spreading the word on obscure anime, comic books, and games whenever possible. Follow him on Twitter @ sageshinigami, or on YouTube @ nolevelcap. Sign in to your CBR account Add Us Summary Generate a summary of this story Like Like follow Follow followed Followed Listen Thread 5 Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap Action RPGs tend to get more attention than their turn-based counterparts, but the turn-based RPG genre has seen a resurgence in recent years. Games like Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 show how dynamic and interesting turn-based combat can be, and older titles like Super Mario RPG have established themselves as classics.

The best turn-based RPGs are surprisingly diverse, telling fantasy stories, sci-fi epics, and even telling tales in the modern world. All of these titles have incredible stories, but the thing that really makes them shine in their combat. These titles are a shining example of how addictive turn-based combat can be.

Image via Konami Suikoden V is one of the underrated classics of the PS2 era of gaming. Set in the Queendom of Falena, Suikoden V follows a prince trying to protect his family after his government is taken over in a coup. As ever with Suikoden games, the focus shifts away from the usual "save the world" plot and focuses on gathering a military capable of restoring peace to the land.

The Suikoden battle systems have always been fun thanks to the variety present. Suikoden V expands on the franchise's usual war battles, forcing players to learn how to wage war both on land and sea at the same time. Meanwhile, the duels remain just as intense as ever, while the six-on-six battles are unlike anything else on the market.

Super Mario RPG screenshot showing Mario, Mallow, Bowser, and Geon standing together while Mallow pointing at something Image via ArtePiazza, Nintendo Super Mario RPG: Legend of the Seven Stars is still just as fun today as it was when it was released. Despite a massive upgrade in graphics, it still retains the charm of the original and its cutesy, diorama settings for each stage. While the story isn't much to think about, the world-building given to the Mushroom Kingdom is awesome.

Though some people describe Super Mario RPG as an ideal RPG for newcomers , Square still managed to innovate with its combat. The timing-based attack and defense forced players to do more than just sit back and select options from a menu.

Players who mastered blocking at the right time could often avoid damage altogether, so there was plenty of incentive to get it right. The remake retains all of this and even adds some fun team attacks to shake things up.

Ochette, Castti, Throne, Osvald, Partitio, Agnea, Hikari, and Temenos at a camp in a forest in Octopath Traveler II Image via Square-Enix Square-Enix tapped into the charm of the 16-bit era with their Octopath Traveler series. Yet there's more to the franchise than being a nostalgic throwback. Octopath Traveler II tells eight stories of eight travelers who just happen to find themselves working together as they overcome major obstacles in their lives.

While the first game is more of a testing ground, the second title improves on every aspect to create a generational classic. The game's combat is exceptional thanks to a mix of the franchise's Boost system and every enemy having specific weaknesses that players have to discover on their own. It naturally encourages players to build a balanced party and commit to big moves to finish enemies off.

Dragon Quest XI battle screenshot showing Erik blocking a high kick from Jade in the martial arts tournament Image via Square-Enix Dragon Quest XI is still the peak of traditional JRPG gaming, years later. It tells what feels like a traditional story early on, but adds enough twists and subversions for players to never really know where it's all going. Though it's one of the longer games out, it's also a delight to play, thanks to the variety in settings and the adorable Toriyama character designs.

The battle system keeps things mostly straightforward, but still builds on ideas from other games. Its "Pep" system allows players to shake things up by giving characters a sudden boost in power and allowing them to unleash their "Pep Powers" on random enemies. Meanwhile, players will find enjoyment in finding which characters support one another the best to be the most powerful in any battle encounter.

Like A Dragon Infinite Wealth Cast showing Chitose, Ichiban, Eric, and Kiryu standing together in front of a resort. Image via Sega Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth proved the Yakuza franchise is still capable of creating some true works of art. Infinite Wealth sees players take over both Ichiban and Kazuma Kiryu as they set out to accomplish different missions in both Ijincho and Hawaii. As ever, Infinite Wealth offers a thought-provoking main narrative, backed by some truly absurd yet unforgettable side quests.

For a franchise that only went turn-based across its last two installments, Infinite Wealth displays a mastery of what makes turn-based combat fun. RGG Studios has created a world where positioning and elemental effects matter just as much as being the right level for a battle. At the same time, its amusing class system makes players want to experiment, just to see what happens next.

Gustave, Maelle, Lune, and Sciel exploring on the island in the early parts of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Image via Sandfall Interactive Though it's far from the savior of turn-based RPGs, there's no doubt that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is one of the best turn-based games ever made. Following a small group of Expeditioners on a quest to fight a cosmic being, Expedition 33 offers a beautiful, heartbreaking story that will stick with fans long after the credits roll.

items.title: Award-winning Expedition 33, BG3 star cancels next role over health concerns
items.summary: Jennifer English will no longer star in the action game Tides of Annihilation
items.content:
Listen Thread 2 Add Us Link copied to clipboard By Tomas Franzese Published Jul 18, 2026, 11:08 AM EDT News Jennifer English will no longer star in the action game Tides of Annihilation

Clair Obscur wins historic sweep at The Game Awards, including Game of the Year What's happening at Xbox? Every change, rumor, and canceled game in a turbulent month All confirmed Marvel characters in Wolverine's PS5 game (so far) Award-winning Expedition 33, BG3 star cancels next role over health concerns Image: The Game Awards Tides of Annihilation Sign in to your Polygon.com account Eclipse Glow Games has announced that Jennifer English, the actress best known for portraying Shadowheart in Baldur's Gate 3 and Maelle in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , will no longer star in Tides of Annihilation .

English had been publicly attached to portray Gwendolyn, the lead character in Tides of Annihilation , since its reveal at a State of Play in Feb. 2025. Just a couple of months later, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 launched, and English won several awards for portraying Maelle in that game. While Gwendolyn was shaping up to be her next major role in a AAA game, Eclipse Glow Games took to X on July 17 to explain that Jennifer English had exited the project due to a health issue.

"We have some sad news to share, that Jennifer English will be stepping away from her role as the English voice of Gwendolyn, the main protagonist in Tides of Annihilation due to personal health reasons," the post explains. "She will continue to support the project in an advisory role. Earlier in June, Jennifer informed us that she would no longer be able to continue in the role. While we were, of course, saddened by her decision, we completely understood and respected Jennfier [sic.] in prioritizing her well-being. We are also deeply grateful for the care and responsibility she showed throughout this process."

English has not elaborated on this further on her personal social media accounts, but she is still attached to a couple of other video game projects at this time. She recently appeared in a video highlighting Emberville's cast and showed up at TennoCon to confirm that she'd be portraying the Empress of Eldveil in Soulframe . Eclipse Glow Games' post even states that she has taken on an advisory role in the game's development and helped the studio find a replacement actress for Gwendolyn.

Tides of Annihilation is part of the recent wave of Chinese action games like Black Myth: Wukong and Blood Message . Unlike those games, which are based on Chinese history, this one is a more Western-inspired fantasy game based on Arthurian legend. The gameplay mostly takes after titles like Devil May Cry, although it also features boss fights against giants that look inspired by Shadow of the Colossus . Tides of Annihilation will be playable at Gamescom this year.

Eclipse Glow Games says the new lead actress will be featured in a public demo that Gamescom attendees will be able to try out in August, although the studio is not yet ready to confirm who the new Gwendolyn is. We also don't know how much this change will impact Tides of Annihilation's development timeline, as it still does not have a release date.

Whenever the game does arrive, it'll be available on PlayStation 5, Windows PC, and Xbox Series X.

Sandfall Interactive's 9 wins eclipses record set by The Last of Us Part 2

items.title: Clair Obscur Actor Pulls Out Of Stunning New Fantasy Game
items.summary: She was set to star in the upcoming Tides of Annihilation
items.content:
Prolific video game actor Jennifer English will no longer play the main hero in Eclipse Glow Games’ upcoming hack-and-slash action-adventure Tides of Annihilation . The development team informed fans on July 17 that Baldur’s Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 actor would have to pull out of voicing protagonist Gwendolyn due to “personal health reasons.”

“She will continue to support the project in an advisory role. Earlier in June, Jennifer informed us that she would no longer be able to continue in the role,” Eclipse Glow Games wrote on X . “While we were, of course, saddened by her decision, we completely understood and respected Jennifer in prioritizing her well-being. We are also deeply grateful for the care and responsibility she showed throughout this process.”

The studio said it has already found a replacement for English with her help, but didn’t reveal who it was. The new actor will appear in the latest version of the game that players go hands-on with at Gamescom 2026 in August. “We have truly enjoyed this journey together, and wish Jennifer all the very best, and most importantly, good health and happiness in whatever comes next,” the development team wrote. “ To our players, thank you for your continued support and understanding. We’re truly grateful to have you on this journey with us.”

English is best known for her role as Shadowheart in Baldur’s Gate 3 and Maelle in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 . She’s also appeared in Elden Ring , Wuthering Waves , and most recently Marathon . Tides of Annihilation was first revealed during a 2025 PlayStation showcase and revolves around elements of Arthurian legend invading modern-day London for an urban fantasy action game that focuses on flashy arcade combat. It’s the latest single-player AAA blockbuster to come out of China, following in the footsteps of games like Black Myth Wukong .

English’s involvement had helped raise the profile of the game. Earlier this year she won a BAFTA for her role in Clair Obscur .

Update 7/18/2026, 5:01 p.m. ET: English has now put out her own statement about taking a step back from Tides of Annihilation .

“Recently, I’ve found myself needing to slow down and reassess the commitments I’ve taken on,” she wrote. “After careful thought, I’ve realized I need to take time to reset and properly look after my mental and physical health. With that in mind, I’ve made the difficulty–yet personally necessary–decision to step away from the project as it moves into its next phase.”

Jennifer English’s message on Instagram about stepping away from Tides of Annihilation to look after her mental and physical health. by u/Caledor152 in pcgaming

items.title: Baldur's Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33's Jennifer English pulls out of fantasy RPG Tides of Annihilation: "Recently, I've found myself needing to slow down"
items.summary: The actor is staying on as an advisor moving forward
items.content:
The actor is staying on as an advisor moving forward

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Eclipse Glow Games) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Baldur's Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 star Jennifer English is pulling out of upcoming fantasy RPG Tides of Annihilation due to personal health reasons, and is handing her role over to another yet-unnamed actor while working as an advisor on the project.

Jennifer English has been involved in some of the best RPGs of recent years, lending her vocal chords to Baldur's Gate 3's Shadowheart, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 's Maelle, Elden Ring's Latenna, and much more. While Tides of Annihilation was hoping to cash in on the genre's lucky charm, things haven't turned out as planned.

"We have some sad news to share, that Jennifer English will be stepping away from her role as the English voice of Gwendolyn, the main protagonist in Tides of Annihilation due to personal health reasons," developer Eclipse Glow Games writes in a social media post. "She will continue to support the project in an advisory role."

We have some sad news to share, that Jennifer English will be stepping away from her role as the English voice of Gwendolyn, the main protagonist in Tides of Annihilation due to personal health reasons. She will continue to support the project in an advisory role. Earlier in… July 18, 2026

"During this transition, Jennifer remained incredibly supportive and, together with other members of our voice cast, helped us identify and connect with talented performers who could carry Gwendolyn forward while staying true to the character," the studio adds. "With Jennifer's support and recommendation, together with recommendations from other members of our voice cast, we've been fortunate to find an incredibly talented performer to carry Gwendolyn forward."

The Tencent -backed Eclipse Glow Games didn't name who will be voicing the game's protagonist moving forward, but we'll apparently find out by the time Tides of Annihilation's Gamescom 2026 demo is in the public's hands: "Players will hear her performance for the first time in the demo this August, as well as in select official updates shared across our channels during the same period."

As Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 further proves the Jen English effect, one co-star wants to join anything she does Baldur's Gate 3 Karlach actor teases more about whether the "key cast" will return for the HBO sequel series Clair Obscur Lune actor would be "delighted" to reprise her role, but she doesn't know of any plans Over on Instagram , English said she's "really grateful to have been part of Tides of Annihilation since the game's inception and to have worked with such lovely team." The actor also shared a bit more about what motivated her to take a step back from the project: "Recently, I've found myself needing to slow down and reassess the commitments I've taken on. After careful thought, I've realised I need to take time to rest and properly look after my mental and physical health."

Tides of Annihilation doesn't have a release window, but it'll be coming to PC, Xbox Series X|S and PS5 when it's out the oven. The game was originally set to have English star opposite her real-life partner Aliona Baranova in a shattered, twisted fantasy version of London.

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

While you're here, why not check out the most exciting new games of 2026 and beyond.

Kaan freelances for various websites including Rock Paper Shotgun, Eurogamer, and this one, Gamesradar. He particularly enjoys writing about spooky indies, throwback RPGs, and anything that's vaguely silly. Also has an English Literature and Film Studies degree that he'll soon forget. items.title: Cosplayers Showcased Alicia, Verso, and Clea from Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - The Dessandr Family of Artists
items.summary: Models Ekaterina "Waters" Kozina, Ilya "Zektornis" Shevchenko, and Anastasia "Svizee" Volkova successfully transformed into Alicia, Verso, and Clea from the role-playing game Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - members of the talented Dessandr family of artists.
items.content:
0 Источник: Официальная группа фотографа Tanuki на VK.com 18 Jul 09:51 Models Ekaterina "Waters" Kozina, Ilya "Zektornis" Shevchenko, and Anastasia "Svizee" Volkova successfully transformed into Alicia, Verso, and Clea from the role-playing game Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - members of the talented Dessandr family of artists.

Photographer Alleria "Tanuki" Gegel published new photos intended for fans of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 who have already played this role-playing game.

During the photoshoot, cosplayers Ekaterina "Waters" Kozina, Ilya "Zektornis" Shevchenko, and Anastasia "Svizee" Volkova appeared as characters such as Alicia, Verso, and Clea.

Clea, Verso, and Alicia are key figures belonging to the mysterious and influential Dessandr family. In the game's world, humanity tries to survive and confronts the mysterious Artist.

At the beginning of the story, their relationship is presented as a complex family drama, full of distrust, unspoken words, and old grievances. They are divided not only ideologically but also physically, being on opposite sides of the conflict in a crumbling world.

Verso takes on the role of a secret protector, Clea acts as a strict and powerful figure pursuing grand goals, and Alicia tries to find a balance between them and restore the lost family legacy.

items.summary: Sandfall Interactive is taking its time before revealing and releasing Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 on Switch 2. The 2025 GOTY is still missing from Nintendo’s…

items.title: Final Fantasy Meets Clair Obscur In New Free RPG
items.summary: This promising new RPG will be released soon.
items.content:
A character from Lord of Mysteries looks forward. By Bruno Yonezawa Published Jul 17, 2026, 12:00 AM EDT Bruno Yonezawa is a Gaming Senior Writer for Screen Rant. He's been practicing professional journalism for over 10 years, and is also a communications specialist. He's played games all his life and turned that passionate hobby into a passionate career, in an industry where he's worked as a writer, editor, analyst, and more. Sign in to your ScreenRant account Add Us Summary Generate a summary of this story Like Like follow Follow followed Followed Thread Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap A new RPG release is on the horizon, mixing elements from both Final Fantasy and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , and it already looks extremely promising if you consider its original source material. Games that are great and popular, such as the two aforementioned ones, tend to serve as inspiration for following titles. For example, there’s a new upcoming RPG inspired by Final Fantasy and Fire Emblem , and it will arrive in August this year.

Now, there’s a new RPG in the making that will bring elements from other well-known RPGs. As was reported by Gameoneer , a new MMORPG, based on a web novel series, is being produced, and it already seems inspired by titles like Final Fantasy and Clair Obscur . The title will be released soon, so you should definitely keep it on your radar.

A character walks the dark streets of a city in Lord of Mysteries. The upcoming RPG release in 2026 is called Lord of Mysteries . The MMORPG is based on the Lord of the Mysteries web series, which was serialized between 2018 and 2020. The story mixes elements of Victorian steampunk with cosmic horror, and the game will feature several classic RPG elements, like pathways, which are the equivalent of classes. The Victorian-era steampunk vibes are highly reminiscent of Clair Obscur and even a bit of the Dishonored series, both of which draw inspiration from the theme.

The new Lord of Mysteries RPG will allow you to pick one of 22 different pathways for your character, including some classic classes, like Warrior and Bard. The developer, SPARK NEXA, has confirmed that all the playable narrative content will be entirely accessible for free, as this is a free-to-play game. Nevertheless, as an MMO, there will be a monetization system revolving around cosmetics and other options, like a battle pass and progression boosts, in this Lord of the Mysteries adaptation .

Two characters relax next to each other in Lord of Mysteries. From its trailer and promotional material so far, Lord of Mysteries is building up to be a gorgeous and intriguing game. With well-refined visuals and a powerful source material to sustain it, the MMORPG looks promising. Luckily, we won’t have to wait too much to start playing.

Lord of Mysteries is slated for release in China on August 21 this year. The game’s global release is expected to happen in 2027, though a more specific release date has yet to be determined. The game will be playable on PC and mobile devices (Android and iOS). For anyone who enjoys deep and dark storytelling with stunning visuals and Victorian-era steampunk themes, this RPG that carries elements of Final Fantasy and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 may be unmissable.

31 9.4 /10 Lord of Mysteries 10 stars 9 stars 8 stars 7 stars 6 stars 5 stars 4 stars 3 stars 2 stars 1 star Like Follow Followed TV-14 Animation Mystery Drama Action Fantasy Release Date June 28, 2025 Network Tencent Video Directors Gideon Liu 7 Images Close Cast See All Yang Kaiqi Klein Moretti Lei Wu Dunn Smith (voice) Where to watch Close WHERE TO WATCH Streaming BUY

items.title: New Official Clair Obscur Release Confirmed for 2027
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 developer Sandfall and its partners showcase a new release focused on Gustave coming in 2027.
items.content:
New Official Clair Obscur Release Confirmed for 2027 From GameRant (Written by Joshua Duckworth) on July 16, 2026 | OpenCritic

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has become one of gaming's biggest success stories over the past year, and fans will be able to revisit it in 2027 with a brand-new release. This new release is focused on the best Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 character Gustave, although fans should be aware that it is on the pricier end.

The award-winning RPG quickly earned a passionate fanbase after its breakout launch, and since then, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has remained on everyone's tongue. Everything from merch to new updates has been on the menu for a while, but now fans have something else to look forward to— Clair Obscur: Arm of Gustave .

Guillaume Broche, director of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, recently made some salient and noteworthy comments about Zelda: Breath of the Wild.

The newest collectible to join the world of Expedition 33 is Clair Obscur: Arm of Gustave, a life-size replica of Gustave's iconic mechanical arm produced by PureArts. Measuring 19 inches long, the premium collectible faithfully recreates one of the game's most recognizable objects with a marble-like polyresin finish, metallic gold accents, rotating cylinders, and a detailed display base covered in flowers. It's one of the most elaborate gaming collectibles...

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 wins Best Game at Develop:Star Awards 2026
items.summary: This year's Develop:Star Award recipient was Monument Valley 3 developer Ustwo Games
items.content:
Monument Valley 3 developer Ustwo Games also receives Develop:Star Award

Image credit: Sandfall Interactive News by Sophie McEvoy Staff Writer Published on July 16, 2026 Follow Kepler Interactive The Develop:Star Awards 2026 were held last night, where Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 continued its success by winning three awards.

Sandfall Interactive's title was recognised for Best Game, Best Narrative, and Best Audio.

Frontier Developments was named Best Studio after launching Jurassic World Evolution 3, while Landfall and Aggro Crab's Peak received Best Original IP.

Both developers also jointly received the Best Small Studio award.

Sony Interactive Entertainment received the Publishing Star award, and Sucker Punch Productions' Ghost of Yōtei won Best Visual Art.

No Man's Sky took home the inaugural Best Ongoing Game award, and FuturLab's PowerWash Simulator was recognised for Best Mobile Game.

This year's Develop:Star Award recipient was Monument Valley 3 developer Ustwo Games.

"Thank you to everyone who joined us for The Develop:Star Awards last night and congratulations to all the winners and those shortlisted," said Tandem Events managing director Andy Lane.

"It was a wonderful evening spent celebrating the very best of the amazing work released over the past year, and the amazing people behind that work. Thank you to our sponsors, everyone who voted, and all those who made the night possible, it was an evening to remember."

The Develop:Star Awards will return next year on Wednesday, July 14, during Develop:Brighton 2027, which will be held from July 13 to July 15.

items.title: "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33" has been awarded Best Game at the 2026 Develop:Star Awards
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 maintained its winning streak by securing three awards at the Develop:Star Awards 2026, which took place last night.
items.content:
Back to news "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33" has been awarded Best Game at the 2026 Develop:Star Awards Image credit: Sandfall Interactive Last night's Develop:Star Awards 2026 saw significant wins for the game "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33," which clinched awards in three categories.

Recognized for its excellence, Sandfall Interactive's game was honored with the awards for Best Game, Best Narrative, and Best Audio.

Awarded as the Best Studio, Frontier Developments was celebrated following the release of Jurassic World Evolution 3. The prize for Best Original IP went to Landfall and Aggro Crab for their game, Peak.

These developers also shared the accolade for Best Small Studio.

Sony Interactive Entertainment took home the Publishing Star award, while Ghost of Yōtei, by Sucker Punch Productions, won for Best Visual Art.

No Man's Sky received the first-ever Best Ongoing Game award, and FuturLab's PowerWash Simulator was distinguished as Best Mobile Game.

The prestigious Develop:Star Award was granted to Ustwo Games, known for Monument Valley 3.

Andy Lane, managing director of Tandem Events, expressed gratitude to the attendees, saying, "Thank you to everyone who joined us for The Develop:Star Awards last night and congratulations to all the winners and those shortlisted."

He added, "It was a wonderful evening spent celebrating the very best of the amazing work released over the past year, and the amazing people behind that work. Thank you to our sponsors, everyone who voted, and all those who made the night possible, it was an evening to remember."

The Develop:Star Awards are set to return on Wednesday, July 14, during the 2027 Develop:Brighton event scheduled from July 13 to 15.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 director thinks devs will eventually "be able to churn out a game in 5 seconds with a prompt," but he hopes they're not any good
items.content:
When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Sandfall Interactive) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 10 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Guillaume Broche, the lead developer behind last year's hugely acclaimed RPG Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, reckons we'll one day be able to prompt an entire game into existence, but that's a theoretical future that's scary for him and many other developers.

Asked about generative AI in the games space during a chat in Konbini's Video Game Club show, Broche clarified that his studio, Sandfall Interactive, isn't using the tech for any "creative" work. When it comes to administrative duties and coding, Broche says "it depends," but generating anything else feels "like it takes away from the joy of creating things."

Watch On Sandfall Interactive found itself in hot water last year when AI-generated textures were found in the final game. The developer swiftly removed the assets in question and explained they were placeholders not intended for release, though the mishap did lead to at least two of its GOTY awards being revoked . It sounds like the studio's next game won't be toying around with the tech in the same way.

"I think everyone's a little scared, though - of the road ahead, of how things might turn out," Broche continues. "With video games, we're still somewhat protected because, technically speaking, it's a different ball game. But I think the day will come when we'll be able to churn out a game in five seconds with a prompt. Will it be a good game? I hope not. But I think everyone's asking themselves the same question - and admitting it's a little scary."

At least, after the huge success of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, Broche and his team don't feel much pressure. "That's why I'm also glad… because we're a studio that's secure. So even if we're not doing things in the best way possible, we don't care."

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 devs were told that "no one" would like one of the RPG's best systems Ex-Assassin's Creed lead says he used AI to learn to code: "It was brutal. ChatGPT kind of sucked." Why so many game developers don't want to use generative AI Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is "sort of the successor" to Final Fantasy creator's overlooked JRPG Lost Odyssey, lead says: "Truly one of the most powerful, emotional experiences of my life"

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

Kaan freelances for various websites including Rock Paper Shotgun, Eurogamer, and this one, Gamesradar. He particularly enjoys writing about spooky indies, throwback RPGs, and anything that's vaguely silly. Also has an English Literature and Film Studies degree that he'll soon forget. items.title: Clair Obscur’s Director Knows Kingdom Hearts II Is The Best One
items.content:
Kingdom Hearts is back in the broader public consciousness (we know it never leaves the minds of sickos) with Kingdom Hearts IV getting a new trailer last month , and that means it’s as good a time as any to remind you that Kingdom Hearts II is the best one . 20 years later, the PS2 classic remains a pitch-perfect summation of the best parts of Square Enix’s Disney and Final Fantasy mash-up. If you don’t want to take my word for it, ask Guillaume Broche, creative director of Game of the Year winner Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 .

Broche appeared on an episode of Jeux Vidéo Club, French YouTube channel Konbini’s show in which people walk through a video game store and tell stories about the games that inspired them. In it, Broche talks about how Kingdom Hearts II is one of his favorite games.

“I think [ Kingdom Hearts II is] in my top 5 games of all time,” he says as translated through Konbini’s video translation. “Truly exceptional. […] There’s really something about that gameplay that I’ve been looking for ever since Kingdom Hearts II . Kingdom Hearts III is pretty good, but it didn’t manage to recapture everything [ II ] did in terms of gameplay, which was exceptional. There was a real depth to it. You could spend 150 hours in the game, actually. Seriously, if you like action RPGs with cool systems, play this game.”

Though Broche recommends anyone play the game if they like action RPGs, Kingdom Hearts is a series that you probably shouldn’t just jump into with the second entry, even if someone says it’s the best in the series. The series’ 20 years of stories are all deeply intertwined, to the point where Kingdom Hearts IV is including characters from games you can’t even play anymore . Yes, Kingdom Hearts II is the best one, but eat your veggies and play the first game and Chain of Memories first.

Don’t miss the latest reviews, news and tips. Sign up for our free newsletter.

Also: Switch 1 stalls out and 2K lays off Project Ethos developers

Reset aims to bring Kepler Interactive's design sensibility to curating stories about how games intersect with other art forms

Housemarque pays homage to Sandfall Interactive’s award-winning RPG

Sandfall Interactive’s RPG is on sale on its anniversary

Sandfall Interactive has pushed through a small patch for the game’s first trip around the sun

The only other game to snag these five major wins was 2023's popular RPG Baldur's Gate 3

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is now available in Arabic!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, is now available with Arabic text! This brings the total number of supported langua
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, is now available with Arabic text!

This brings the total number of supported languages to 20!

With the new Arabic language support, we had to consider additional changes to the game’s UI to ensure a smooth and enjoyable experience for Arabic-speaking players around the world.

Adding Arabic localization has been a true team effort over many months, from the incredible adaptation and localization work carried out by the linguists, to reworking the vast majority of the user interface for right-to-left reading, all the way through localization testing.

Every team involved brought passion and dedication to making this possible, and we couldn’t be more grateful for their work. We hope this update makes the story feel even more personal to our players.

Today’s update also includes some additional fixes and improvements. Read the full patch notes here!

Thank you all so much for your continued support! <3

Tomorrow comes.

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Patch v1.5.6 is now live!
items.summary: Expeditioners, Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is now available in Arabic! Content New supported language (text): Arabic [/*] Credi
items.content:
Expeditioners,

Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is now available in Arabic!

Content

  • New supported language (text): Arabic

    [/*]

  • Credits updated (new linguists, testers and fonts)

    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where applying burn after an enemy has been stunned causes them to be able to act immediately after recovering from the stun.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Cleim weapon level 10 Lumina effect that does not trigger correctly on every instance of area of effect skills.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Martenon’s Lumina effects that do not work if Sciel does not have two consecutive turns.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where Sireso weapon, Level 4 Lumina, and Powerful buff, from Pictos/Luminas, applied at the start of battle may override each other depending on the character's position in combat.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Chromatic Lampmaster's AOE skill missing a sound cue on the jump attack.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Sirene's Widow Ballet missing SFX during second charm attack.

    [/*]

  • Fixed characters in combat that may have displaced heads in Photo Mode if they were not seen while turning on Photo Mode.

    [/*]

  • Various minor fixes of the UI and localizations.

    [/*]

We are aware of a crash affecting some of you when loading into the Reacher or going out of the Manor. This crash is related to some specific PC configurations. Until we fix this issue, you can work around it by setting ‘Global Illumination’ to anything below ‘Epic’ temporarily.

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - We'll continue monitoring the reports coming in and working on future fixes!

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Dispatch Bundle
items.summary: Expeditioners! Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dispatch are now available together in a 10% off bundle! Bundle art by Derek Stratton This is a really special bundle for us - since its release, Dispatch left such an impact on us and immediat
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dispatch are now available together in a 10% off bundle! 

Bundle art by Derek Stratton

This is a really special bundle for us - since its release, Dispatch left such an impact on us and immediately became one of our favourites.   

Our games are quite different, but we couldn’t help ourselves - and ultimately, we just want more people to discover the powerful stories and artistry behind these games.   

So in the words of our Creative Director, Guillaume Broche: “Play Dispatch, it’s very very cool”

Save 10% off both games by purchasing this bundle!

items.title: 1st Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
items.summary: Expeditioners!! Today marks 1 year since we released Clair Obscur: Expedition 33. This past year has been an incredible journey and so we have a few things to share with you today. To celebrate the occasion, the team put together a special
items.content:
Expeditioners!! 

 

Today marks 1 year since we released Clair Obscur: Expedition 33. This past year has been an incredible journey and so we have a few things to share with you today. 

 

To celebrate the occasion, the team put together a special piece of artwork that we hope you will enjoy! 

8 Million Copies Sold

We're so thrilled, and honestly still quite overwhelmed, to announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has officially reached 8 million copies sold!

Thank you so so much to every single person who has played, created art, shared, streamed, discussed, and supported this game!!

Game Update: New Haircuts + Patch Notes

We've pushed a fun little update live today: new haircuts are now available in-game, giving the expedition even more style to strut around in! 💅 

For the full list of changes, fixes, and improvements, you can read the patch notes here

Community Emotes

Our community is packed with phenomenally talented artists and we wanted to celebrate that today.  

We commissioned some of our amazing community creators to design official Expedition 33 emotes for you to use in your own channels! 

A massive thank you to IrunoArt, comickergirl (Sarah Leuver), and Bugyakis for their brilliant work on each of these!!

They're FREE to download and use here

Official Baguette Merch

In the mood to add some baguette to your wardrobe? 

We’ve partnered with Orcival and Le Béret Français to bring you the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 striped shirt and beret for your cosplays! 🥖    You can purchase the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Orcival mariniere here

And the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Le Béret Français beret is available to buy here

On top of that, keep an eye out for a limited number of units of the Monolith Set to be back in stock from our partners at Laced and Pix n' Love soon!! 

And Finally… Cake

It's been a big day for us. Thank you for making it possible. 🎂 

And if you know someone who wants to pick up the game, we're running a 20% sale right now on Steam - As well as some fun merchandise goodies available on our merch page!

Merch Page

From the bottom of our hearts, we’re so thankful to each and every one of you – this has been the best year of our lives, thanks to you. 

 

- The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Patch v1.5.5 is now LIVE!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Happy 1 Year Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33!! The details of this patch are written out below: New content Added 1st Anniversary haircuts for the expedition! Gustave's Anniversary haircut is available at the Gestr
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Happy 1 Year Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33!! The details of this patch are written out below:

New content

  • Added 1st Anniversary haircuts for the expedition!

    • Gustave's Anniversary haircut is available at the Gestral Merchant near Stone Wave Cliffs.

      [/*]

    • Verso + Maelle + Lune + Sciel + Monoco's Anniversary haircuts are available at the Gestral Merchant near Grosse Tête on the World Map.

      [/*]
    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where Danseuse outfit for Lune and Sciel made the character menu too obscur.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where it was not possible to open a rest point in various locations when an in-world dialogue was playing.

    [/*]

  • Fixed collision issues in various locations.

    [/*]

  • Adjusted some achievement text for consistency.

    • We've noticed something went wrong after adjusting this, we're investigating now to resolve the issue

      [/*]

    • Fixed the issue! We've readjusted some of the achievement descriptions back to the correct achievements. Thank you for your patience!!

      [/*]
    [/*]

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - and most importantly, thank you for all of your support this entire year!! We wish you a wonderful anniversary day and hope you enjoy some cake to celebrate with us!

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Patch v1.5.3 is live!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Thank you for all of your reports - the details for the fixes in this patch are written out below: Update On Sirene Crash After a thorough internal investigation, the crash that occurred after defeating Sirene can no longer b
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Thank you for all of your reports - the details for the fixes in this patch are written out below:

Update On Sirene Crash

  • After a thorough internal investigation, the crash that occurred after defeating Sirene can no longer be reproduced on our end. If you keep getting this issue, we’d suggest updating your Windows to the latest version. If the issue persists, please submit a report and we’ll continue investigating.

    [/*]

Bug Fixes & Improvements

Photo Mode

  • Fixed facial hair of characters being blurred in the Photo Mode on the close distance if Shading was set to Epic.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in Photo mode that prevented the Inner Cone Angle and Outer Cone Angle options from affecting the shape of the preview light source.

    [/*]

  • Photo Mode's spotlight and point light display has been modified to improve their visibility

    [/*]

Gameplay

  • Fixed an issue where loading a save while picking up a journal could cause that journal to disappear permanently and become impossible to collect again.

    • Missing journals are now also restored retroactively when loading affected saves.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Fixed a soft lock that could occur, during the Chromatic Glissando encounter in Sirene’s Dress level, when a dead expeditioner equipped with Shortcut and Second Chance pictos was swallowed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on Gestral Beach where using the Rematch option after losing the volleyball minigame could create a blocked save.

    [/*]

  • Fixed a visual issue where characters affected by Inverted would not have the correct colour effect applied to their eyes.

    [/*]

Environment

  • Fixed various environment issues (collisions, misplaced assets, missing or incorrect footsteps SFX) across Verso's Drafts, the World Map, and other levels.

    [/*]

  • Fixed various lighting issues on Steam Deck in Verso’s Drafts, Endless Tower and Stone Wave Cliffs levels.

    [/*]

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - We'll continue monitoring the reports coming in and working on future fixes!

~ The Expedition 33 Team
